sources: BallDontLie PGA API (GOAT tier — players, field, odds, props, stats)

players.name: Scottie Scheffler
players.notes: Elite ball-striker. Best SG:Approach on Tour. Premium plays: strokes under, birdies over.

players.name: Jon Rahm
players.notes: 2023 Masters champion. LIV — major invitee.

players.name: Xander Schauffele
players.notes: Most well-rounded player on Tour. No weakness. Excels at Torrey Pines.

anomalies.note: confScore 72.2 but odds +12000 — likely value in placement markets

players.name: Collin Morikawa
players.notes: Second-best iron player. Low ball flight suits firm/windy. Premium bogey under play.

anomalies.note: confScore 71.4 but odds +5300 — likely value in placement markets

players.name: Rory McIlroy
players.notes: Best driver on Tour. High birdie ceiling but volatile. Great for matchups.

players.name: Patrick Cantlay
players.notes: Elite putter. Lowest bogey rate. Best bogey under play on Tour. Riviera specialist.

players.name: Tommy Fleetwood
players.notes: Links-style player. Low ball flight suits windy conditions. Good bogey under candidate.

anomalies.note: confScore 65.7 but odds +500000 — likely value in placement markets

players.name: Cameron Smith
players.notes: 2022 Open Championship winner. Elite putter. LIV — major invitee via past champion/OWGR.

players.name: Brooks Koepka
players.notes: 4x major champion. LIV — major invitee.

players.name: Ludvig Aberg
players.notes: Young star with elite power. Short game still developing. High upside.

players.name: Viktor Hovland
players.notes: Great ball-striker but short game liability. Negative SG:ARG. Risky bogey unders.

players.name: Bryson DeChambeau
players.notes: 2024 US Open champion. Power game. LIV — major invitee via past champion/OWGR.

players.name: Tyrrell Hatton
players.notes: Ryder Cup star, multiple DP World Tour wins. LIV — major invitee via OWGR.

players.name: Shane Lowry
players.notes: Links specialist. Open champion. Low ball flight dominates in wind. Best in coastal/windy conditions.

anomalies.note: confScore 62.8 but odds +200000 — likely value in placement markets

players.name: Hideki Matsuyama
players.notes: Masters champion. Elite approach play with high ball flight. Streaky putter — hot weeks can win anywhere.

players.name: Dustin Johnson
players.notes: 2020 Masters champion. LIV — major invitee.

players.name: Jason Day
players.notes: Resurgent veteran. Short game wizard when healthy. Bay Hill specialist. Good at TPC Sawgrass.

anomalies.note: confScore 62.1 but odds +500000 — likely value in placement markets

players.name: Joaquin Niemann
players.notes: Chilean bomber, long off tee. LIV — major invitee via OWGR.

players.name: Wyndham Clark
players.notes: Powerful driver. Higher bogey rate offsets birdies. Volatile for props.

anomalies.note: confScore 60.9 but odds +27500 — likely value in placement markets

players.name: Sam Burns
players.notes: Talented ball-striker with streaky putting. When putter is hot, can contend anywhere.

anomalies.note: confScore 60.9 but odds +115000 — likely value in placement markets

players.name: Russell Henley
players.notes: Extremely accurate. Low bogey rate. Great at precision courses like Harbour Town and Colonial.

anomalies.note: confScore 60.5 but odds +8600 — likely value in placement markets

players.name: Patrick Reed
players.notes: 2018 Masters champion. LIV — major invitee.

players.name: Sungjae Im
players.notes: Iron man — plays every week. Rarely misses cuts. Great for MC and top 20 props.

anomalies.note: confScore 60 but odds +145000 — likely value in placement markets

players.name: Max Homa
players.notes: Solid all-around. Riviera and Torrey specialist. Reliable at home courses.

players.name: Corey Conners
players.notes: Elite iron player but can't putt. Highest GIR with lowest conversion. Great approach stats, fade putting props.

players.name: Denny McCarthy
players.notes: Best putter on Tour. Short off the tee. Needs accuracy courses where length doesn't matter. Elite bogey under.

players.name: Byeong Hun An
players.notes: Consistent ball-striker. No elite category but no major weakness. Reliable for top-20 finishes.

players.name: Adam Scott
players.notes: Veteran with elite swing. Augusta specialist. Still competitive but ceiling has lowered.

players.name: Matt Fitzpatrick
players.notes: US Open champion. Precision player. Not long but very accurate. Thrives on tight, demanding courses.

players.name: Keegan Bradley
players.notes: Steady veteran. Consistent without being flashy. Good MC and top-20 candidate.

players.name: Sahith Theegala
players.notes: High birdie upside but high bogey rate. Very aggressive. Best for birdie overs.

players.name: Sepp Straka
players.notes: Steady ball-striker from Austria. No standout category. Reliable for MC and top-20 at mid-strength events.

players.name: Chris Kirk
players.notes: Comeback story. Steady and consistent. Good at shorter accuracy courses. Low variance player.

players.name: Tony Finau
players.notes: Elite power but inconsistent approach and flat-stick. Best at bombers courses. Fade candidate at short tracks.

players.name: Aaron Rai
players.notes: Precise iron player. Accuracy over power. Good at courses that demand shotmaking. Steady for props.

players.name: Tom Kim
players.notes: Young talent with flair. Aggressive player with high birdie ceiling. Inconsistent but fun for props.

players.name: Si Woo Kim
players.notes: Versatile Korean player with multiple wins. Solid all-around game. Steady at Augusta but ceiling is top-10. Reliable MC prop.

players.name: Louis Oosthuizen
players.notes: 2010 Open champion. Silky ball-striker, multiple runner-up finishes in majors. LIV — major invitee.

players.name: Brian Harman
players.notes: Open champion. Lefty with great short game. Not a power player. Thrives on accuracy courses and links.

players.name: Billy Horschel
players.notes: East Lake specialist. TPC Sawgrass has history. Emotional player — performs well when locked in.

players.name: Davis Thompson
players.notes: Young steady player. Won his first Tour event. No standout skill but consistent. Good for MC props.

players.name: Christiaan Bezuidenhout
players.notes: South African with smooth swing. Accuracy player. Good in wind. Putting holds him back from contending.

players.name: Sergio Garcia
players.notes: 2017 champion. One of Augusta's great history makers — 23 prior top-10s. Putter remains the weakness. Respect the track record.

players.name: Robert MacIntyre
players.notes: Scottish lefty with links pedigree. Gritty competitor. Good in wind. Putter can go cold.

players.name: Justin Thomas
players.notes: Former world #1 in a slump. Ball-striking still elite but putter has gone cold. High ceiling, low floor.

players.name: Cameron Young
players.notes: Huge power but accuracy issues. Multiple runner-up finishes. Volatile scorer — birdie overs at long courses.

players.name: Akshay Bhatia
players.notes: Young lefty talent. Aggressive. Putting holds him back. High birdie ceiling with high floor.

players.name: Nicolai Hojgaard
players.notes: Danish twin (brother Rasmus also on Tour). Athletic and powerful. Still adapting to PGA Tour courses.

players.name: Austin Eckroat
players.notes: Oklahoma product with power. Still developing consistency. Better at longer courses. Thin field value play.

players.name: Harris English
players.notes: Steady veteran. Good all-around game without elite skill. East Lake familiarity. Reliable for MC at full fields.

players.name: Stephan Jaeger
players.notes: German journeyman having a solid stretch. Shot 58 on mini-tour. Reliable for MC at weaker fields.

players.name: Davis Riley
players.notes: Alabama native still adjusting to major speed. Solid iron player. Mid-tier Augusta floor. Better at more forgiving venues.

players.name: Taylor Montgomery
players.notes: Consistent mid-tier player. Augusta debut was rough. Power game but needs precision Augusta demands. Watch form going in.

players.name: Seamus Power
players.notes: Irish power player. Better on links and European-style tracks. Augusta is not his best fit but competitive.

players.name: Adam Hadwin
players.notes: Canadian accuracy player. Very consistent but lacks the elite ballstriking Augusta demands at the top. MC value.

players.name: Mackenzie Hughes
players.notes: Canadian precision player. Limited Augusta experience. Consistent MC candidate. No contender ceiling yet.

players.name: Lucas Glover
players.notes: US Open champion (2009). Augusta veteran who plays with grinder mentality. Consistent MC maker. T8 here proves he can compete.

players.name: Rasmus Hojgaard
players.notes: Danish twin with DP World Tour pedigree. Solid debut at Augusta. European-style patience suits the course. Rising stock.

players.name: Min Woo Lee
players.notes: Australian talent with power. Sister Minjee is LPGA star. Inconsistent but can go low. Good for T20 at mid-tier.

players.name: J.T. Poston
players.notes: Ball-striking specialist. Consistent mid-field at Augusta. No major breakthrough yet. Solid MC prop.

players.name: Taylor Pendrith
players.notes: Bomber who relies on length. Weak short game. Best at wide-open bombers courses. Fade at precision tracks.

players.name: Jordan Spieth
players.notes: 3x major champ in a slump. Course history still matters — elite at Augusta, Colonial, Pebble. Buy low candidate.

players.name: Nick Dunlap
players.notes: Won as amateur on Tour. Young talent still developing. High variance — DFS dart throw at big courses.

players.name: Ben Griffin
players.notes: Steady mid-tier player. No outstanding skill. MC candidate at weaker fields. Fade at marquee events.

players.name: Justin Rose
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Ryan Gerard
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Andrew Novak
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Michael Brennan
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Michael Thorbjornsen
players.notes: Promising rookie from Stanford. Athletic and long. Raw but talented. Watch for breakout at long courses.

players.name: Erik van Rooyen
players.notes: South African power player. Long off the tee but short game lets him down. Augusta demands scrambling he doesn't have.

players.name: Ryan Fox
players.notes: New Zealand bomber. Son of Grant Fox. Huge off the tee but Augusta short game demands more. Better on longer bombers tracks.

players.name: Lee Hodges
players.notes: Alabama native making his Masters bones. Good ballstriker still learning major speeds. Development player.

players.name: J.J. Spaun
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Kurt Kitayama
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Nick Taylor
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Jake Knapp
players.notes: Longest hitter on Tour. Accuracy is a problem. Short game is Tour-worst tier. Only play at bomber-friendly tracks.

players.name: Brian Campbell
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Keith Mitchell
players.notes: Power player with accuracy issues. Thrives at wide courses. Short game liability.

players.name: Eric Cole
players.notes: Big hitter with inconsistent short game. Best at wide-open courses. Avoid at precision tracks.

players.name: Callum Shinkwin
players.notes: English DP World Tour player. Augusta debut was rough. Links-style game. Not Augusta-suited.

players.name: Thriston Lawrence
players.notes: South African DP World Tour player. Augusta debut. Solid European game but long road to contend here.

players.name: Chris Gotterup
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Jacob Bridgeman
players.notes: Former Clemson standout. Athletic young player building tour experience. High ceiling but still raw.

players.name: Marco Penge
players.notes: English DP World Tour graduate. Steady but unspectacular. Still building PGA Tour résumé.

players.name: Maverick McNealy
players.notes: Stanford product. West Coast familiarity helps. Pebble and Torrey specialist. Thin field MC candidate.

players.name: Phil Mickelson
players.notes: 3x Masters champion (2004, 2006, 2010). Physically declining but Augusta knowledge is unmatched. Career MC prop only.

players.name: Ricky Castillo
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Danny Willett
players.notes: 2016 champion in stunning Jordan Spieth collapse. Inconsistent since. Pedigree at Augusta — can't fully fade the champion.

players.name: Bubba Watson
players.notes: 2x champion (2012, 2014). Retired from PGA Tour. Invited as champion. Big fade — not competitive anymore.

players.name: Mike Weir
players.notes: 2003 champion. Playing on Champions Tour. Augusta invite is honorary at this stage. MC prop only.

players.name: Zach Johnson
players.notes: 2007 champion. Precision iron player who thrives at Augusta. Former Ryder Cup captain. Fade for outright; MC solid.

players.name: Tiger Woods
players.notes: 5x champion (1997, 2001, 2002, 2005, 2019). NOT PLAYING 2026 — ongoing injury recovery. Course fit reflects current physical condition, not peak-era legacy. Do not use for any props or sim.

players.name: Fred Couples
players.notes: 1992 champion. Champions Tour legend. Ball never short of 12th hole (famous). Playing as a past champ — for the love of the game.

players.name: Bernhard Langer
players.notes: 2x champion (1985, 1993). Augusta legend. Playing as a 68-year-old past champion. Beloved but not a betting proposition. Avoid all props.

players.name: Larry Mize
players.notes: 1987 champion — famous chip-in on 11 in playoff vs Norman. Augusta resident. Honorary start only at this stage. Beloved local.

cutPrediction.note: Augusta historical avg cut: +2.3. Average field this week.

news.headline: Fitzpatrick rides 'lucky' shot to 63, up 1 shot at RBC Heritage
news.description: Matt Fitzpatrick acknowledged getting "lucky" avoiding the water on the par-3 14th and capitalized by holing a 30-foot birdie, the highlight of his second-round 63 on Friday for a one-shot lead at the RBC Heritage.

news.headline: LIV Golf CEO says league has financial commitment to finish season
news.description: Amid questions about the future of LIV Golf, CEO Scott O'Neil told TNT Sports that the league has a financial commitment to finish the 2026 season.

news.headline: Bernhard Langer, 68, breaks age to share Senior PGA Championship lead
news.description: The ageless Bernhard Langer is at it again. The 68-year-old German had a double bogey and still broke his age by two shots with a 66 in the Senior PGA Championship.

news.headline: Ludvig Aberg shoots 63 for 1-shot lead at RBC Heritage
news.description: Ludvig Aberg has the lead at the RBC Heritage with a bogey-free 63 at Harbour Town.

news.headline: LIV Golf is still going, but its days seem numbered and probably always were
news.description: LIV made a lot of noise, caused some nervous days at the PGA Tour and even forced some positive changes. But ultimately, it never did enough to make a real challenge.

news.headline: Max Homa, asked about Garcia, says angry golfers look spoiled
news.description: Asked about the antics like Sergio Garcia's outburst at the Masters, Max Homa said it casts a bad look on professional golfers but also acknowledged that it's a sport that can stir up those emotions.

news.headline: Tiger Woods fights subpoena for prescription drug records
news.description: Tiger Woods' attorney is fighting prosecutors' attempts to subpoena the 15-time major champion's prescription drug records from a pharmacy, according to records.

news.headline: How to bet the RBC Heritage: Best bets, DFS tips and more
news.description: All of the traits bettors should be looking for at the RBC Heritage, and the best bets to make.

news.headline: How to watch PGA's 2026 RBC Heritage on ESPN
news.description: It's time for the PGA's 2026 RBC Heritage. Check out more information on who will be playing and how to catch the action on the ESPN App.

news.headline: Answering some big questions for the rest of the golf season
news.description: What can we expect next from Rory McIlroy? Who disappointed at the Masters? Will Scottie Scheffler add to his major total this year? We answer some big questions following the 2026 Masters.

news.headline: Sergio Garcia apologizes for outburst amid final round of Masters
news.description: Sergio Garcia issued an apology on social media Tuesday for his outburst during his final round of play at the Masters on Sunday.

news.headline: Scottie Scheffler 4-1 or shorter to win each of the next four golf majors, Rory McIlroy next
news.description: Odds for next four golf majors: 2026 PGA Championship, U.S. Open and the Open Championship, 2027 Masters.

news.headline: Stephen A. praises Rory's poise in Masters win
news.description: Stephen A. Smith was impressed with Rory McIlroy's ability to rise to the moment on Sunday after blowing his lead at Augusta the day before.

news.headline: Rory McIlroy: Good things come to those who wait
news.description: Rory McIlroy believes "good things come to those who wait" after claiming back to back Masters titles.

news.headline: 'I don't make it easy': How Rory McIlroy won back-to-back Masters
news.description: After seeing a six-shot lead erased Saturday and falling behind early Sunday, McIlroy still found a way to secure his second green jacket.﻿
